chunks.text: Course: EE 375 | EECS at UC Berkeley EE 375. Teaching Techniques for Electrical Engineering Catalog Description: Discussion of effective teaching techniques. Use of educational objectives, alternative forms of instruction, and proven techniques to enhance student learning. This course is intended to orient new student instructors to more effectively teach courses offered by the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. Units: 2 Also Offered As: ELENG 375 Prerequisites: Teaching assistant or graduate student Formats: Fall: 1.5 hours of seminar per week Spring: 1.5 hours of seminar per week Final Exam Status: No Class Schedule (Spring 2026): EE 375 – Chunlei Liu Class homepage on bCourses Class Notes EECS/CS Depts manually enrolls UCS2 hires during the adustment period. The Google form will be emailed to you with your offer. This course follows a guerrilla schedule that meets heavily for the first 2 weeks. Attendance is mandatory. Students can contact the faculty for a code to enroll. Non-EECS/CS students will need to wait till week 1 of classes to see if there is room to enroll. This course follows a guerrilla schedule that meets heavily for the first 2 weeks. Attendance is mandatory.
